Reasoning:
The current visit record primarily details diagnoses and management related to gastrointestinal issues (peptic ulcer, cirrhosis, hepatitis C, esophageal varices, alcohol abuse in remission) and associated complications (anemia, analgesic adverse effects). While the patient has underlying liver disease (cirrhosis), which can be associated with lipid abnormalities, there is no explicit evidence in the provided diagnoses or management plan (e.g., specific lab results indicating hypercholesterolemia or hyperlipidemia, or a direct diagnosis of a lipid disorder) to strongly and explicitly predict that "Disorders of Lipid Metabolism" will be coded in the next hospital visit. The management focuses on stabilizing the GI and liver conditions rather than actively managing a primary lipid disorder.
